text: When you focus deeply on a problem, a distinctive set of neural events unfolds. The default mode network — associated with mind-wandering, self-referential thought, and rumination — is suppressed. Simultaneously, the dorsal attention network, anchored in the intraparietal sulcus and frontal eye fields, becomes dominant. This is not passive silence but active competition: the two networks are anticorrelated, and engagement of one systematically suppresses the other. Psychologist Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi described the experiential correlate of this state as 'flow' — a complete absorption in which the sense of self temporarily disappears. Attention, at its most complete, consumes its own witness.
question: According to the passage, what happens to the default mode network during states of deep focus?

options.text: It synchronizes with the dorsal attention network to amplify cognitive performance
It activates to support self-referential processing needed for sustained effort
It is actively suppressed due to its anticorrelated relationship with the dorsal attention network
It transfers its activity to the prefrontal cortex, which maintains executive control
